notes: Boron carbide (B4C). Density 2.52 g/cm³ — lightest armor ceramic. Knoop hardness ~3700 HK (30 GPa) — hardest ceramic. RHAe ~4.0-5.0 per mm vs KE standalone tile. Higher with backing (ESAPI/XSAPI arrays ~5.0). Used in M1A2 SEP v2 turret behind DU inserts. Highest areal density efficiency but most expensive. Degrades under extremely high pressure (shock-induced amorphization at ~25 GPa). Fracture toughness K1c ~2.5-3.5 MPa·m^½.

source.reference: ARL ceramic armor research literature, NIST materials database
source.methodology: RHAe from ARL ballistic test data on B4C standalone tiles vs AP threats, normalized to RHA thickness. Knoop hardness and fracture toughness from published materials science characterization.
